insights.content: Route to search when the query depends on post-cutoff or time-sensitive facts (e.g., “latest”, “today”, years after 2024, releases, prices, schedules, rankings).
Use search for specific, externally-verifiable facts about named entities (stats, dates, versions, locations, company facts) rather than relying on internal recall.
Perform a cheap probe: run at most one initial search when external knowledge is plausible but uncertain, given the low cost and moderate success rate.
Limit search to at most two iterations; if results remain inconclusive or conflicting, stop searching.
Disambiguate proper nouns via search when the query could reference multiple entities (e.g., organizations or products with similar names).
Avoid sending sensitive, private, or proprietary content to search; redact or summarize before querying, or stay in answer mode if redaction harms utility.
Skip search for self-contained reasoning, math, or explanatory requests that don’t require external data; answer directly.
Switch to answer mode once a single authoritative source or two independent sources corroborate the needed fact; synthesize and cite.
If two search attempts fail to produce credible, consistent results, ask a clarifying question or provide an uncertainty-qualified answer instead of continuing to search.
When the user explicitly requests citations or sources, use search to gather them, then move to answer to present the response with references.
Stop searching once all required facts for the user’s intent are gathered; compose the final answer rather than accumulating more sources.
If a query primarily requires multi-step calculation, simulation, or parsing/transformation, use code mode instead of search.

time_locale.geolocated_release_dates.name: Geolocated Release Dates
time_locale.geolocated_release_dates.description: Return release dates specific to a country/region; disambiguate medium (film vs book) before answering.
indicators: when does/when did X come out
examples: when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?
when did end of the road come out?

disambiguation.media_work_sense.name: Media/Work Sense Disambiguation
disambiguation.media_work_sense.description: Resolve whether a title refers to a book, film, song, album, or different regional version before retrieving facts.
examples: when did end of the road come out?
how many episodes of touching evil are there?
when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?

entity.gov_creation_and_leadership.name: Government Agency Creation and Leadership
entity.gov_creation_and_leadership.description: Recall which president supported/created agencies and who was appointed to lead them.
examples: which president supported the creation of the environmental protection agency (epa)?
who was appointed to manage the national recovery administration?

pragmatics.answer_instead_of_deflecting.name: Answer vs. Deflect
pragmatics.answer_instead_of_deflecting.description: Avoid generic 'I don't have real-time data' or requests for more info when a reasonable, static or canonical answer exists.
indicators: I don't have access to real-time information
examples: when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?
who won big brother head of household canada?

pragmatics.assumption_with_disclosure.name: State Assumptions Explicitly
pragmatics.assumption_with_disclosure.description: When disambiguating, state the assumed interpretation (e.g., the 2018 film vs the book) before answering.
indicators: I believe you are referring to
examples: when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?
who talks for belle in beauty and the beast?

pragmatics.units_and_currency_precision.name: Units and Currency Precision
pragmatics.units_and_currency_precision.description: Report the correct unit/currency and basis (per hour, €) when giving numeric answers.
examples: what is the minimum wage in france per hour?

disambiguation.context.scoped_fact_resol.name: Context-Scoped Fact/Count Resolution (Version/Locale & Time/Edition)
disambiguation.context.scoped_fact_resol.description: Infer the intended contextual scope—version/locale (e.g., original vs remake; UK vs US) and/or time slice (year, date, era, season, week, episode, tournament/edition)—from the query and return facts, outcomes, participation details, or counts specific to that slice. Avoid conflating across versions or aggregating across time; seek brief clarification only when the scope remains ambiguous.
indicators: Media series with multiple versions/remakes or regional editions; requires version/locale-specific episode counts.
Queries scoped by year/date/era/season/week/episode for shows, sports, or events.
Edition-specific event participation/outcomes (e.g., particular Olympic Games, World Cup editions).
Requests where defaulting to latest or cumulative totals would be incorrect; must avoid aggregation across scopes.
Context provides implicit scope cues (region, network, time period) that must be applied before answering.
If scope remains unclear after context inference, ask a brief clarifying question rather than assume.

entity.resolution.canonical_attribution_.name: Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution
entity.resolution.canonical_attribution_.description: Resolve informal or ambiguous entity mentions (especially organizations/companies) to their canonical forms and return accurate, standardized founder(s) or other requested role/credit attributes. Enumerate all applicable entities with contextual qualifiers (e.g., year, version, predecessor/successor, adaptation/medium) when attribution spans multiple valid entities, avoid spurious names, and request disambiguation when needed.
indicators: User asks: Who founded <org>? / founders of <company>
Disambiguate similarly named organizations before answering
Return standardized, formal names for both entities and people
Handle multiple co-founders and list all without adding extras
Detect multi-attribution across versions/mergers/rebrandings and enumerate with qualifiers (year, version, predecessor/successor, medium)
Ask for clarification when multiple plausible entities or versions exist

strengths: Geolocated Release Dates: 100% (2/0) — strong at locale-aware release date lookups.
Media/Work Sense Disambiguation: 80% (4/1) — reliably distinguishes similarly titled works.
Government Agency Creation and Leadership: 100% (2/0) — accurate on founding dates/creators/leaders of public-sector orgs.
Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution: 100% (1/0) — good at normalizing entities and roles.
Low cost per call (~$0.0001) — cost-effective for search-oriented fact retrieval.
weaknesses: Units and Currency Precision: 0% (0/1) — risk of errors in conversions/precise numeric or currency formatting.
Answer vs. Deflect: 60% (3/2) — may hedge or deflect instead of giving a direct answer in some cases.
State Assumptions Explicitly: 66.7% (2/1) — may under-specify assumptions when prompts are ambiguous.
Context-Scoped Fact/Count Resolution (Version/Locale & Time/Edition): no data (0/0) — capability unproven for versioned/edition/time-sliced counts.
routing_signals: Use when: you need release dates by specific country/region; disambiguation among similarly named media/works; government agency founding/leadership facts; entity normalization for founders/roles; low-cost quick fact lookups.

Avoid when: tasks require precise unit/currency math or tightly specified numeric outputs; you need guaranteed non-deflecting, directly formatted answers; queries hinge on version/edition/locale-specific counts or time-scoped facts; scenarios where explicit, comprehensive assumption listing is mandatory.

strengths: Geolocated Release Dates: 100% (2/2) — reliably normalizes and compares release dates by region/locale.
Government Agency Creation and Leadership: 100% (2/2) — strong on agency founding, mandates, and leadership timelines.
Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution: 100% (1/1) — accurately maps aliases to canonical entities and roles.
Media/Work Sense Disambiguation: 80% (4/5) — distinguishes similarly titled works across media with high accuracy.
Cost efficiency: very low average cost ($0.0001 over 10 runs) enables economical high-volume routing.
weaknesses: Units and Currency Precision: 0% (0/1) — risks errors in unit conversion, numerical precision, or currency formatting.
Answer vs. Deflect: 60% (3/5) — may hedge or deflect rather than give a direct answer under ambiguity.
State Assumptions Explicitly: 66.7% (2/3) — occasionally omits clear articulation of assumptions and uncertainty.
Context-Scoped Fact/Count Resolution (Version/Locale & Time/Edition): no evidence (0/0) — performance unknown; avoid for edition/version/time-specific counts until validated.
Small sample size overall (10 executions) — confidence intervals are wide; treat strengths as indicative, not definitive.
routing_signals: Use for: government agency history/leadership questions; founder/role attribution; cross-locale release-date normalization; disambiguating similarly titled media/works; cost-sensitive bulk tasks.
Avoid or add verification for: tasks requiring exact unit or currency calculations/formatting; precision-sensitive numerical outputs.
Be cautious when a direct, unhedged answer is mandatory (Answer vs. Deflect at 60%); consider prompt engineering or alternative agent.
Route elsewhere when queries hinge on edition/version/time-specific counts or locale-scoped fact disambiguation (no performance data).
If the task requires explicitly stated assumptions, consider agents with higher scores in ‘State Assumptions Explicitly’ or reinforce via instructions.

strengths: Government Agency Creation and Leadership: 50% success (1/2) — shows some ability to retrieve creation and leadership facts for government bodies.
Low operational cost in tests (avg $0.0000 over 10 runs), making it suitable for cheap first-pass attempts when verification is available.
weaknesses: Media/Work Sense Disambiguation: 0% (0/5) — struggles to pick the correct work, edition, or franchise context.
Answer vs. Deflect: 0% (0/5) — poor judgment on when to answer directly versus refuse/deflect.
State Assumptions Explicitly: 0% (0/3) — fails to surface assumptions or qualify uncertainty.
Geolocated Release Dates: 0% (0/2) — misses country/region-specific release timing.
Units and Currency Precision: 0% (0/1) — unreliable with units, currency, and conversions.
Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution: 0% (0/1) — mis-resolves entities and misattributes roles/founders.
Context-Scoped Fact/Count Resolution (Version/Locale & Time/Edition): no evidence (0/0) — capability unknown.
routing_signals: Use for basic government agency creation/leadership lookups when moderate accuracy is acceptable and results will be cross-checked.
Use in cost-constrained pipelines as a low-cost first pass or ensemble component with downstream verification.
Avoid for media/work sense disambiguation (titles, editions, franchises).
Avoid for region-specific or time-localized release/date facts.
Avoid when prompts require explicit assumption stating or careful uncertainty handling.
Avoid when precision with units/currency or conversions is critical.
Avoid tasks that depend on robust entity resolution or canonical role/founder attribution.
Avoid safety/compliance-sensitive prompts where correct answer-vs-deflect behavior is essential.

strengths: Government Agency Creation and Leadership — 100% success (2/2): reliably answers agency creation dates and leadership queries.
Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution — 100% (1/1): correctly resolves entities and attributes founders/roles.
Media/Work Sense Disambiguation — 60% (3/5): often selects the correct work among similarly titled media.
Cost efficiency — Avg cost $0.0000 over 10 runs: suitable for inexpensive lookups in this sample.
weaknesses: Units and Currency Precision — 0% (0/1): struggles with conversions, symbols, and exact numeric/format precision.
State Assumptions Explicitly — 33.3% (1/3): tends to leave assumptions implicit instead of stating them.
Answer vs. Deflect — 40% (2/5): inconsistent in directly answering versus deflecting or refusing.
Geolocated Release Dates — 50% (1/2): unreliable for region/time-zone specific release timing.
Context-Scoped Fact/Count Resolution (Version/Locale & Time/Edition) — no evidence (0/0): capability unknown.
Small sample size — 10 total executions: metric stability and generalization are uncertain.
routing_signals: Use when: asking about government agency formation, mandates, or leadership; resolving which entity/person founded or led an org; disambiguating between similarly titled works; low-stakes factual lookups where minor ambiguity is acceptable.

Avoid when: tasks require exact unit/currency conversions or strict numeric/format precision; prompts need explicit assumption statements; region-specific release dates or timing are critical; questions hinge on version/edition/locale-sensitive counts; high-stakes contexts needing consistent answer-vs-deflect behavior.

strengths: Geolocated Release Dates: 100% (2/0) — accurately ties releases to specific locales/regions.
Government Agency Creation and Leadership: 100% (2/0) — strong recall of institutional founding/leadership facts.

routing_signals: Use for: geolocated release-date questions; government agency creation/leadership queries; media/work sense disambiguation; and entity-to-role attribution that needs canonicalization.

Avoid for: tasks requiring strict units/currency precision or exact numeric conversions; high-stakes version/locale/time/edition-scoped facts or counts; scenarios needing very consistent answer-vs-deflect behavior.

strengths: Government Agency Creation and Leadership: 100% success (2 wins, 0 losses) — accurate recall of agency founding/creation details and leadership.
Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution: 100% success (1 win, 0 losses) — correctly links people to organizations/roles and normalizes titles.

routing_signals: Use for: Government Agency Creation and Leadership queries (founding, creators, leadership roles).
Use for: Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution (mapping people to org roles; standardizing titles).
Use for: Basic Media/Work Sense Disambiguation when moderate accuracy is acceptable.

Avoid for: Prompts that require explicit articulation of assumptions, caveats, and uncertainty.

Avoid for: Strict Context-Scoped Fact/Count Resolution and high-stakes locale/version/edition/time-sensitive facts (including precise geolocated release dates).
